---
title: "02/04 2026"
output:
  workflowr::wflow_html:
    code_folding: hide
    toc: true
date: "2026-02-04"
---

```{r, echo=F, message=F, warning=F, results=F}
library(rprojroot)
root <- rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file()
source(file.path(root, "analysis/Rfunctions.R"))
root <- rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file()
```


```{r, message=F, warning=F, results=F}
precision=read_xlsx((file.path(root, "../2026/202601/Nava/Data for Nava's thesis/Precision_Nava.xlsx")))
trueness=read_xlsx((file.path(root, "../2026/202601/Nava/Data for Nava's thesis/Trueness_Nava.xlsx")))


precision <- precision %>%
  mutate(
    Position = factor(Position),
    Jaw = factor(Jaw),
    Site = factor(Site)
  )

```

## precision 


### anova test 


```{r, message=F, warning=F}
library(purrr)
library(broom)

# Outcomes
outcomes <- c(
  "Angle", "Base3D", "MDbase", "OLbase", "ApicalBase",
  "Tip3d", "MDtip", "OLtip", "ApicalTip"
)


# Predictors for separate ANOVA
predictors <- c("Position", "Jaw", "Site")

############################################################
# 1️⃣ Run ANOVAs separately for each predictor
############################################################

run_anova_separate <- function(var, predictor) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste(var, "~", predictor))
  model <- aov(formula, data = precision)
  tidy(model) %>%
    filter(term != "Residuals") %>%
    select(term, p.value) %>%
    mutate(Outcome = var,
           Predictor = predictor)
}

# Loop over outcomes and predictors
anova_table <- map_dfr(outcomes, function(var) {
  map_dfr(predictors, function(pred) {
    run_anova_separate(var, pred)
  })
})

# Pivot for readability: Outcomes × Predictors
anova_table_wide <- anova_table %>%
  select(Outcome, Predictor, p.value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Predictor, values_from = p.value)

anova_table_wide$Position=pvalue_adjust(anova_table_wide$Position)
anova_table_wide$Jaw=pvalue_adjust(anova_table_wide$Jaw)
anova_table_wide$Site=pvalue_adjust(anova_table_wide$Site)

anova_table_wide%>%
datatable(extensions = 'Buttons',
          caption = "anova p values", 
            options = list(dom = 'Blfrtip',
                           buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf', 'print'),
                           lengthMenu = list(c(10,25,50,-1),
                                             c(10,25,50,"All"))))

```



```{r, message=F, warning=F}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(rstatix)

# 1. Define measurements
measurements <- c("Angle", "Base3D", "MDbase", "OLbase", "ApicalBase", 
                  "Tip3d", "MDtip", "OLtip", "ApicalTip")

# 2. Corrected function
get_detailed_pairwise <- function(data, group_col) {
  data %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(measurements), names_to = "Measurement", values_to = "Value") %>%
    group_by(Measurement) %>%
    # Use t_test for all pairs
    t_test(as.formula(paste("Value ~", group_col)), detailed = TRUE) %>%
    # NOW adjust the p-values for multiple comparisons
    adjust_pvalue(method = "bonferroni") %>%
    add_significance() %>%
    # Rename 'estimate' to 'mean_diff' for clarity
    mutate(mean_diff = round(estimate,4)) %>%
    # Select columns that are guaranteed to exist now
    select(Measurement, group1, group2, 
           mean_diff, conf.low, conf.high, 
           p, p.adj, p.adj.signif)
}

# --- Execute for all factors ---

results_position <- get_detailed_pairwise(precision, "Position")
results_jaw      <- get_detailed_pairwise(precision, "Jaw")
results_site     <- get_detailed_pairwise(precision, "Site")
################
results_position$conf.low=round(results_position$conf.low,4)
results_position$conf.high=round(results_position$conf.high,4)
results_position$p=pvalue_adjust(results_position$p)
results_position$p.adj=pvalue_adjust(results_position$p.adj)

results_position%>%
datatable(extensions = 'Buttons',
          caption = "position", 
            options = list(dom = 'Blfrtip',
                           buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf', 'print'),
                           lengthMenu = list(c(10,25,50,-1),
                                             c(10,25,50,"All"))))

#################
results_jaw$conf.low=round(results_jaw$conf.low,4)
results_jaw$conf.high=round(results_jaw$conf.high,4)
results_jaw$p=pvalue_adjust(results_jaw$p)
results_jaw$p.adj=pvalue_adjust(results_jaw$p.adj)

results_jaw%>%
datatable(extensions = 'Buttons',
          caption = "jaw", 
            options = list(dom = 'Blfrtip',
                           buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf', 'print'),
                           lengthMenu = list(c(10,25,50,-1),
                                             c(10,25,50,"All"))))

#################
results_site$conf.low=round(results_site$conf.low,4)
results_site$conf.high=round(results_site$conf.high,4)
results_site$p=pvalue_adjust(results_site$p)
results_site$p.adj=pvalue_adjust(results_site$p.adj)

results_site%>%
datatable(extensions = 'Buttons',
          caption = "site", 
            options = list(dom = 'Blfrtip',
                           buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf', 'print'),
                           lengthMenu = list(c(10,25,50,-1),
                                             c(10,25,50,"All"))))
```



* P-value Range Symbol 
+ $0.05 < P \le 1.00$ ns No significant difference
+ $0.01 < P \le 0.05$ * Significant
+ $0.001 < P \le 0.01$**  Very Significant
+ $0.0001 < P \le 0.001$   *** Extremely Significant
+ $P \le 0.0001$ ****Extremely Significant



## trueness


### anova test 


```{r, message=F, warning=F}
library(purrr)
library(broom)

# Outcomes
outcomes <- c(
  "Angle", "Crest", "MBcrest", "DLcrest", "Verticalcrest", "Tip", "MBtip", "DLtip", "Verticaltip" 
)


# Predictors for separate ANOVA
predictors <- c("Site", "Jaw", "Space", "Jawspace")

############################################################
# 1️⃣ Run ANOVAs separately for each predictor
############################################################

run_anova_separate <- function(var, predictor) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste(var, "~", predictor))
  model <- aov(formula, data = trueness)
  tidy(model) %>%
    filter(term != "Residuals") %>%
    select(term, p.value) %>%
    mutate(Outcome = var,
           Predictor = predictor)
}

# Loop over outcomes and predictors
anova_table <- map_dfr(outcomes, function(var) {
  map_dfr(predictors, function(pred) {
    run_anova_separate(var, pred)
  })
})

# Pivot for readability: Outcomes × Predictors
anova_table_wide <- anova_table %>%
  select(Outcome, Predictor, p.value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Predictor, values_from = p.value)

anova_table_wide$Site=pvalue_adjust(anova_table_wide$Site)
anova_table_wide$Jaw=pvalue_adjust(anova_table_wide$Jaw)
anova_table_wide$Space=pvalue_adjust(anova_table_wide$Space)
anova_table_wide$Jawspace=pvalue_adjust(anova_table_wide$Jawspace)

anova_table_wide%>%
datatable(extensions = 'Buttons',
          caption = "anova p values", 
            options = list(dom = 'Blfrtip',
                           buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf', 'print'),
                           lengthMenu = list(c(10,25,50,-1),
                                             c(10,25,50,"All"))))

```


### pairwise comparisons


```{r, message=F, warning=F}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(rstatix)

# 1. Define measurements
measurements <- c(
  "Angle", "Crest", "MBcrest", "DLcrest", "Verticalcrest", "Tip", "MBtip", "DLtip", "Verticaltip" 
)

# 2. Corrected function
get_detailed_pairwise <- function(data, group_col) {
  data %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(measurements), names_to = "Measurement", values_to = "Value") %>%
    group_by(Measurement) %>%
    # Use t_test for all pairs
    t_test(as.formula(paste("Value ~", group_col)), detailed = TRUE) %>%
    # NOW adjust the p-values for multiple comparisons
    adjust_pvalue(method = "bonferroni") %>%
    add_significance() %>%
    # Rename 'estimate' to 'mean_diff' for clarity
    mutate(mean_diff = round(estimate,4)) %>%
    # Select columns that are guaranteed to exist now
    select(Measurement, group1, group2, 
           mean_diff, conf.low, conf.high, 
           p, p.adj, p.adj.signif)
}

# --- Execute for all factors ---

results_site <- get_detailed_pairwise(trueness, "Site")
results_jaw      <- get_detailed_pairwise(trueness, "Jaw")
results_space     <- get_detailed_pairwise(trueness, "Space")
results_jawspace     <- get_detailed_pairwise(trueness, "Jawspace")
################
results_space$conf.low=round(results_space$conf.low,4)
results_space$conf.high=round(results_space$conf.high,4)
results_space$p=pvalue_adjust(results_space$p)
results_space$p.adj=pvalue_adjust(results_space$p.adj)

results_space%>%
datatable(extensions = 'Buttons',
          caption = "space", 
            options = list(dom = 'Blfrtip',
                           buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf', 'print'),
                           lengthMenu = list(c(10,25,50,-1),
                                             c(10,25,50,"All"))))

################
results_jawspace$conf.low=round(results_jawspace$conf.low,4)
results_jawspace$conf.high=round(results_jawspace$conf.high,4)
results_jawspace$p=pvalue_adjust(results_jawspace$p)
results_jawspace$p.adj=pvalue_adjust(results_jawspace$p.adj)

results_space%>%
datatable(extensions = 'Buttons',
          caption = "jawspace", 
            options = list(dom = 'Blfrtip',
                           buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf', 'print'),
                           lengthMenu = list(c(10,25,50,-1),
                                             c(10,25,50,"All"))))

#################
results_jaw$conf.low=round(results_jaw$conf.low,4)
results_jaw$conf.high=round(results_jaw$conf.high,4)
results_jaw$p=pvalue_adjust(results_jaw$p)
results_jaw$p.adj=pvalue_adjust(results_jaw$p.adj)

results_jaw%>%
datatable(extensions = 'Buttons',
          caption = "jaw", 
            options = list(dom = 'Blfrtip',
                           buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf', 'print'),
                           lengthMenu = list(c(10,25,50,-1),
                                             c(10,25,50,"All"))))

#################
results_site$conf.low=round(results_site$conf.low,4)
results_site$conf.high=round(results_site$conf.high,4)
results_site$p=pvalue_adjust(results_site$p)
results_site$p.adj=pvalue_adjust(results_site$p.adj)

results_site%>%
datatable(extensions = 'Buttons',
          caption = "site", 
            options = list(dom = 'Blfrtip',
                           buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf', 'print'),
                           lengthMenu = list(c(10,25,50,-1),
                                             c(10,25,50,"All"))))
```
